channel.title: plum.science Lab News
channel.description: Lab news from the Plum Lab

item.title: Germline-encoded recognition of peanut underlies development of convergent antibodies in humans
item.description: Humans may be genetically programmed to produce antibodies against peanut, suggests a #ScienceTranslationalMedicine study showing nonallergic people harbor convergent, germline-encoded antibodies against the allergen Ara h 2. https://scim.ag/3LqfEp9
item.content:encoded:
Humans may be genetically programmed to produce antibodies against peanut, suggests a #ScienceTranslationalMedicine study showing nonallergic people harbor convergent, germline-encoded antibodies against the allergen Ara h 2. https://scim.ag/3LqfEp9

This paper by Marini-Rapoport et al. reveals that human IgG recognition of the immunodominant peanut allergen Ara h 2 is intrinsically programmed by the germline antibody repertoire, driven by structural convergence across diverse, highly prevalent gene rearrangements that target a common public epitope in both allergic and non-allergic individuals.

item.title: In a #ScienceImmunology Review from earlier this year, researchers discuss how
item.description: In a #ScienceImmunology Review from earlier this year, researchers discuss how interactions between the nervous and immune systems could impact neurological disorders and allergy-related behaviors like food avoidance. https://scim.ag/3Y1hcsr
item.content:encoded: In a #ScienceImmunology Review from earlier this year, researchers discuss how interactions between the nervous and immune systems could impact neurological disorders and allergy-related behaviors like food avoidance. https://scim.ag/3Y1hcsr

item.title: An anti-allergy vaccine protects mice from anaphylaxis for as long as one year,
item.description: This paper by Conde et al. demonstrates that a conjugate vaccine coupling a mutated human IgE fragment to a CRM carrier induces durable, neutralizing anti-IgE autoantibodies in humanized mice, providing long-term protection against systemic and cutaneous anaphylaxis without compromising anti-helminth immunity. #ScienceTranslationalMedicine. https://scim.ag/3XxHAdi
item.content:encoded: This paper by Conde et al. demonstrates that a conjugate vaccine coupling a mutated human IgE fragment to a CRM carrier induces durable, neutralizing anti-IgE autoantibodies in humanized mice, providing long-term protection against systemic and cutaneous anaphylaxis without compromising anti-helminth immunity. #ScienceTranslationalMedicine. https://scim.ag/3XxHAdi

item.title: Central neural circuits underlying itch sensation — a Review by Yan-Gang

item.title: Nature research paper: Maternal stress triggers early-life eczema through fetal
item.description: Nature research paper: Maternal stress triggers early-life eczema through fetal mast cell programming go.nature.com/3UPz8Vm
item.content:encoded:
Nature research paper: Maternal stress triggers early-life eczema through fetal mast cell programming

go.nature.com/3UPz8Vm

item.title: The causes of early-life eczema have been unclear, but evidence indicates that
item.description: The causes of early-life eczema have been unclear, but evidence indicates that changes to fetal immune cells and sensory neurons during pregnancy play a key part go.nature.com/45Y9aUQ
item.content:encoded:
The causes of early-life eczema have been unclear, but evidence indicates that changes to fetal immune cells and sensory neurons during pregnancy play a key part

go.nature.com/45Y9aUQ

item.title: The sneezing, itchy eyes and coughing elicited by some allergens are caused by proteins creating holes in airway cells.
item.description: This paper by Shi et al. (reported by Nature) identifies that unrelated allergens trigger allergic airway inflammation across species via conserved pore-forming proteins that create membrane holes, allowing calcium influx to initiate the primary danger signals that drive the type 2 immune response. go.nature.com/3H9B1cd
item.content:encoded:
This paper by Shi et al. (reported by Nature) identifies that unrelated allergens trigger allergic airway inflammation across species via conserved pore-forming proteins that create membrane holes, allowing calcium influx to initiate the primary danger signals that drive the type 2 immune response.

go.nature.com/3H9B1cd

item.title: Mast cells regulate the brain-dura interface and CSF dynamics
item.description: Excited to share our new work, led by an exceptional graduate student, Tornike Mamuladze, on the role of meningeal mast cells in regulation of brain/dura access points, with implications to CSF flow dynamics and meningitis || www.cell.com/cell/fulltex...
item.content:encoded:
Excited to share our new work, led by an exceptional graduate student, Tornike Mamuladze, on the role of meningeal mast cells in regulation of brain/dura access points, with implications to CSF flow dynamics and meningitis || www.cell.com/cell/fulltex...

This paper by Mamuladze et al. reveals that dural mast cells regulate cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) dynamics at arachnoid cuff exit (ACE) points, where degranulation-induced histamine release dilates bridging veins to restrict CSF flow, thereby recruiting neutrophils and forming a critical barrier that prevents pathogens from spreading into the brain during meningitis.

item.title: A speedy imaging method can map the nerves running from a mouse’s brain and
item.description: A speedy imaging method can map the nerves running from a mouse’s brain and spinal cord to the rest of its body at micrometre-scale resolution go.nature.com/3Il0e3x
item.content:encoded:
A speedy imaging method can map the nerves running from a mouse’s brain and spinal cord to the rest of its body at micrometre-scale resolution

go.nature.com/3Il0e3x

item.title: Online now: Long-lived IgE plasma cells that reside in the spleen contribute
item.description: Online now: Long-lived IgE plasma cells that reside in the spleen contribute to the persistence of the IgE response
item.content:encoded: Online now: Long-lived IgE plasma cells that reside in the spleen contribute to the persistence of the IgE response

item.title: In a new #ScienceImmunology Review, researchers discuss how interactions
item.description: In a new #ScienceImmunology Review by the Molofsky lab, researchers discuss how interactions between the nervous and immune systems could impact neurological disorders and allergy-related behaviors like food avoidance. scim.ag/4mpgzE5
item.content:encoded: In a new #ScienceImmunology Review by the Molofsky lab, researchers discuss how interactions between the nervous and immune systems could impact neurological disorders and allergy-related behaviors like food avoidance. scim.ag/4mpgzE5

item.title: DOCK8 in T cells promotes Th17 and Treg cell functionality to restrain mucosal mast cells and limit susceptibility to oral anaphylaxis
item.description: This paper by Janssen et al. demonstrates that DOCK8 deficiency drives food allergy and oral anaphylaxis by disrupting T regulatory cell and Th17 function, causing dysbiosis and an IL-25/IL-4 cascade that expands mucosal mast cells and increases intestinal permeability.
item.content:encoded: This paper by Janssen et al. demonstrates that DOCK8 deficiency drives food allergy and oral anaphylaxis by disrupting T regulatory cell and Th17 function, causing dysbiosis and an IL-25/IL-4 cascade that expands mucosal mast cells and increases intestinal permeability.

item.title: 🚨New paper with @thexavierlab.bsky.social @science.org on the critical role of
item.description: 🚨New paper with @thexavierlab.bsky.social @science.org on the critical role of 🔄neuroimmune crosstalk in immune defense in the gut🪱🪱.🧪@genelayinstitute.bsky.social @mgbresearch.bsky.social @harvardmed.bsky.social @broadinstitute.org www.science.org/doi/10.1126/...
item.content:encoded:
🚨New paper with @thexavierlab.bsky.social @science.org on the critical role of 🔄neuroimmune crosstalk in immune defense in the gut🪱🪱.
🧪@genelayinstitute.bsky.social @mgbresearch.bsky.social @harvardmed.bsky.social @broadinstitute.org
www.science.org/doi/10.1126/...

item.title: The sensory neuroimmune frontier
item.description: This review by Brian Kim and David Artis details how peripheral sensory neuroimmunology has emerged as a distinct field by revealing critical interactions between the immune and sensory nervous systems that govern allergy, infection, autoimmunity, and tissue homeostasis.
item.content:encoded: This review by Brian Kim and David Artis details how peripheral sensory neuroimmunology has emerged as a distinct field by revealing critical interactions between the immune and sensory nervous systems that govern allergy, infection, autoimmunity, and tissue homeostasis.

item.title: Why scratching makes a rash worse and a potential benefit to scratching--it
item.description: Why scratching makes a rash worse and a potential benefit to scratching--it reduced S. aureus on skin. Andrew Liu's paper from our lab is out now at #science #neuroimmune #immunology www.science.org/doi/10.1126/...
item.content:encoded: Why scratching makes a rash worse and a potential benefit to scratching--it reduced S. aureus on skin. Andrew Liu's paper from our lab is out now at #science #neuroimmune #immunology www.science.org/doi/10.1126/...

item.title: Scratching a mosquito bite can offer a moment of bliss, and now scientists have
item.description: Scratching a mosquito bite can offer a moment of bliss, and now scientists have learnt why: scratching activates an immune response that helps to protect the skin against harmful infections, at least in mice. https://go.nature.com/4aJpL0r
item.content:encoded:
Scratching a mosquito bite can offer a moment of bliss, and now scientists have learnt why: scratching activates an immune response that helps to protect the skin against harmful infections, at least in mice.

https://go.nature.com/4aJpL0r

item.title: The neuroimmune axis: Integrating two systems to decipher itch
item.description: This review by Kothari and Dong outlines how bidirectional neuroimmune crosstalk—where immune signaling molecules directly activate sensory neurons and neuropeptides modulate local immune responses—drives pathologic itch, highlighting tissue-specific mechanisms and novel targetable pathways for pruritic diseases.
item.content:encoded: This review by Kothari and Dong outlines how bidirectional neuroimmune crosstalk—where immune signaling molecules directly activate sensory neurons and neuropeptides modulate local immune responses—drives pathologic itch, highlighting tissue-specific mechanisms and novel targetable pathways for pruritic diseases.

item.title: Neuroanatomical organization of electroacupuncture in modulating gastric function in mice and humans
item.description: This paper by Dong et al. demonstrates that electroacupuncture drives a somatosensory-vagal-gastric reflex to improve gastric motility in both mice and human patients via the activation of deep fascial nociceptors and gastro-projecting vagal motor neurons.
item.content:encoded: This paper by Dong et al. demonstrates that electroacupuncture drives a somatosensory-vagal-gastric reflex to improve gastric motility in both mice and human patients via the activation of deep fascial nociceptors and gastro-projecting vagal motor neurons.

item.title: Wired for immunity: neuroimmune control of the lung by sensory neurons — a
item.description: Wired for immunity: neuroimmune control of the lung by sensory neurons — a Review by Anna M. Ehlers, Idaira M. Guerrero-Fonseca, Christophe Altier, Bryan G. Yipp & Sebastien Talbot #neuroscience #neuroskyence www.nature.com/articles/s41...
item.content:encoded:
Wired for immunity: neuroimmune control of the lung by sensory neurons — a Review by Anna M. Ehlers, Idaira M. Guerrero-Fonseca, Christophe Altier, Bryan G. Yipp & Sebastien Talbot

#neuroscience #neuroskyence

www.nature.com/articles/s41...
